title: Method and apparatus for skipping uplink transmission in mobile communications
abstract: Various solutions for skipping uplink transmission with respect to user equipment and network apparatus in mobile communications are described. An apparatus may determine whether a padding buffer status report (BSR) is included in a protocol data unit (PDU). The apparatus may skip transmission of the PDU when the padding BSR is included in the PDU. The PDU may comprise a medium access control (MAC) PDU. The MAC PDU may comprise no MAC service data unit (SDU).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: determining, by a processor of an apparatus, whether a padding buffer status report (BSR) is included in a first protocol data unit (PDU); and skipping, by the processor, transmission of the first PDU when the padding BSR is included in the first PDU. 2. The method of Claim 1 , wherein the first PDU comprises a medium access control (MAC) PDU, and wherein the MAC PDU comprises no MAC service data unit (SDU). 3. The method of Claim 1 , further comprising: receiving, by the processor, a configured grant; and skipping, by the processor, transmission of the first PDU on the configured grant. 4. The method of Claim 1 , further comprising: determining, by the processor, whether a periodic BSR is included in a second PDU; determining, by the processor, whether data is available in a logical channel; and 

 skipping, by the processor, transmission of the second PDU when the periodic BSR is included in the second PDU and no data is available in the logical channel. 5. The method of Claim 1 , further comprising: determining, by the processor, whether a periodic BSR is included in a second PDU; determining, by the processor, whether data is available in a logical channel; and transmitting, by the processor, the second PDU when the periodic BSR is included in the second PDU and the data is available in the logical channel, wherein the second PDU comprises solely the periodic BSR. 6. The method of Claim 5, further comprising: receiving, by the processor, a configured grant; and transmitting, by the processor, the second PDU on the configured grant. 7. The method of Claim 1 , further comprising: receiving, by the processor, a logical channel restriction; determining, by the processor, a restricted logical channel according to the logical channel restriction; determining, by the processor, that data is available in the restricted logical channel; and skipping, by the processor, transmission of the first PDU when no data is included in the first PDU. 8. The method of Claim 1 , further comprising: receiving, by the processor, a configuration to enable uplink transmission skipping. 9. The method of Claim 8, wherein the configuration is received via a radio resource control (RRC) signaling or a layer 1 (L1 ) signaling. 10. The method of Claim 1 , wherein the skipping comprises not generating the first PDU. 11. An apparatus, comprising: a transceiver capable of wirelessly communicating with a plurality of nodes of a wireless network; and a processor communicatively coupled to the transceiver, the processor capable of: determining whether a padding buffer status report (BSR) is included in a first protocol data unit (PDU); and skipping transmission of the first PDU when the padding BSR is included in the first PDU. 12. The apparatus of Claim 1 1 , wherein the first PDU comprises a medium access control (MAC) PDU, and wherein the MAC PDU comprises no MAC service data unit (SDU). 13. The apparatus of Claim 1 1 , wherein the processor is further capable of: receiving, via the transceiver, a configured grant; and skipping transmission of the first PDU on the configured grant. 14. The apparatus of Claim 1 1 , wherein the processor is further capable of: determining whether a periodic BSR is included in a second PDU; determining whether data is available in a logical channel; and skipping transmission of the second PDU when the periodic BSR is included in the second PDU and no data is available in the logical channel. 15. The apparatus of Claim 1 1 , wherein the processor is further capable of: determining whether a periodic BSR is included in a second PDU; determining whether data is available in a logical channel; and transmitting, via the transceiver, the second PDU when the periodic BSR is included in the second PDU and the data is available in the logical channel, wherein the second PDU comprises solely the periodic BSR. 16. The apparatus of Claim 15, wherein the processor is further capable of: receiving, via the transceiver, a configured grant; and 

 transmitting, via the transceiver, the second PDU on the configured grant. 17. The apparatus of Claim 1 1 , wherein the processor is further capable of: receiving, via the transceiver, a logical channel restriction; determining a restricted logical channel according to the logical channel restriction; determining that data is available in the restricted logical channel; and skipping transmission of the first PDU when no data is included in the first PDU. 18. The apparatus of Claim 1 1 , wherein the processor is further capable of: receiving, via the transceiver, a configuration to enable uplink transmission skipping. 19. The apparatus of Claim 18, wherein the configuration is received via a radio resource control (RRC) signaling or a layer 1 (L1 ) signaling. 20. The apparatus of Claim 1 1 , wherein, in skipping transmission of the first PDU, the processor is capable of not generating the first PDU.
